title: After-Sales Service & Client Care Protocol APAC
content:
Aurelle After-Sales Service & Client Care — APAC Protocol

SERVICE AS A RELATIONSHIP TOOL
After-sales service in the luxury context is not a cost center — it is one of the most powerful relationship touchpoints available. A client who brings their 10-year-old LOVE bracelet in for polishing and leaves with it gleaming and a personal note from their stylist is a client who will make their next significant purchase at Aurelle. The service interaction must be treated with the same intentionality as a new sale.

SERVICE CENTER NETWORK APAC
- Singapore Service Centre (SSC): Primary regional service hub for Southeast Asia, India, Australia. Workshop capacity: 45 watchmakers, 12 jewellery craftspeople. TAT (turnaround time) target: 3 weeks standard, 5 weeks for complex complications.
- Tokyo Service Centre: Dedicated to Japan market. Highest capacity per market — 28 watchmakers, 8 jewellery craftspeople. TAT: 2 weeks standard (Japan client expectations are most stringent for service timelines).
- Shanghai Service Centre: Mainland China and Taiwan. Regulatory requirement: all repairs on China-duty-paid items must be serviced domestically or documented through official customs channels for border repair. TAT: 3–4 weeks.
- Seoul Workshop: South Korea, recently upgraded. 18 watchmakers, 6 jewellery craftspeople. TAT: 2.5 weeks standard.
- Hong Kong Workshop: HK and Macau. Also handles high-complexity HJ restoration. TAT: 3 weeks.
- Certificate of Service: Issued for all watch services — records movement condition, parts replaced, water resistance test result, power reserve test. Adds to the piece's provenance and value.

WATCH SERVICE STANDARDS
Recommended full service intervals (communicated to clients at point of sale):
- Quartz movements: 3–5 years (battery, seal, timing check)
- Automatic movements: 5–7 years (full movement service, lubrication, gasket replacement)
- High complications: 7–10 years (manufacturer recommends service at Aurelle only)

Client communication on service: Stylist proactively initiates service reminder based on purchase date recorded in CRM. Do not wait for client to complain of malfunction — preventive outreach builds trust.

Warranty:
- International warranty: 2 years from purchase on all watches (movement and parts, excludes physical damage, water damage beyond rated depth, battery)
- Extended service plan: Aurelle Prestige Care (optional, sold at point of purchase): 5-year extension, includes 1 complimentary full service and priority TAT.
- For Tier 1 clients: complimentary watch service as per segmentation policy (DOC-003

title: APAC After-Sales Service & Repair Operations Policy
content:
Aurelle APAC — After-Sales Service (SAV) & Repair Operations Policy\n\n
  
  ## Strategic Role of After-Sales Service\n
  After-Sales Service (SAV) is a primary client retention and 
  brand equity lever for Aurelle APAC. Industry benchmarking 
  confirms that clients with positive SAV experiences demonstrate 
  28% higher 3-year repurchase rates than non-SAV clients. 
  All SAV interactions must be treated as VIC engagement 
  opportunities and logged in CRM with the same rigor as 
  purchase transactions.\n\n
  
  ## APAC SAV Network Structure\n
  
  ### Certified Service Centres (CSC)\n
  Aurelle operates **7 Certified Service Centres** across APAC, 
  each capable of performing both watches and jewellery servicing:\n
  - **Shanghai CSC** (North Asia hub) — Full-service: movements, 
  case restoration, gem re-setting, engraving.\n
  - **Tokyo CSC** (Ginza) — Watches specialization + fine 
  jewellery. Highest technical capacity in APAC.\n
  - **Hong Kong CSC** (Elements Mall) — Covers HK, Macau, 
  and overflow from South China.\n
  - **Seoul CSC** (Cheongdam) — Watches and jewellery. 
  Korea-specific lacquer and enamel dial restoration capability.\n
  - **Singapore CSC** — Covers Singapore, SEA markets 
  (Thailand, Philippines routed via SDC logistics).\n
  - **Sydney CSC** — Covers Australia market only.\n
  - **Delhi CSC** — Covers India. Limited to jewellery 
  servicing and basic watch strap/case polishing. 
  Complex movements shipped to Tokyo CSC.\n\n
  
  ### Remote Market SAV Routing\n
  Markets without a local CSC (Philippines, Thailand, 
  Taiwan, Macau) follow this routing protocol:\n
  - Client deposits item at boutique.\n
  - Boutique team issues SAV receipt with estimated 
  timeline and generates CRM SAV ticket.\n
  - Item transported via insured courier to nearest CSC 
  (Singapore for SEA; Hong Kong for Taiwan/Macau).\n
  - Standard turnaround target: **21 business days** 
  door-to-door for remote markets.\n\n
  
  ## Service Level Standards\n
  
  | Service Type              | Target TAT (Turnaround) | VIC Priority TAT |\n
  |---------------------------|------------------------|------------------|\n
  | Watch battery replacement | 48 hours in-boutique   | Same day         |\n
  | Strap replacement/resize  | 24 hours in-boutique   | Same day         |\n
  | Polish & refinish         | 7 business days (CSC)  | 5 business days  |\n
  | Movement service (full)   | 28 business days       | 18 business days |\n
  | High Jewellery re-setting | 35 business days       | 25 business days |\n
  | Bespoke engraving         | 10 business days       | 7 business days  |\n
  | Major complication repair | 60 business days       | 45 business days |\n\n
  
  ## VIC SAV Protocol (Tier 1 — Maison VIP)\n
  - Dedicated SAV concierge assigned for all Maison VIP clients. 
  Concierge provides proactive status updates every 5 business days.\n
  - White-glove pickup and return delivery service offered 
  at no charge for items valued above USD 10,000.\n
  - Complimentary loan watch offered (from boutique loan fleet) 
  for all watch service durations exceeding 14 business days.\n
  - SAV event: Upon return of item, boutique director or 
  senior stylist personally presents the serviced item with 
  a detailed service report card. Champagne service offered 
  as standard.\n\n
  
  ## Warranty Policy\n
  - **Watches**: 4-year international warranty from date of 
  purchase covering manufacturing defects. Excludes damage 
  from misuse, water ingress beyond rated depth, and 
  unauthorized servicing.\n
  - **Jewellery**: 2-year warranty covering prong integrity 
  and clasp mechanisms. Stone loss due to setting failure 
  within warranty period covered at no charge.\n
  - Warranty claims must be validated via CRM purchase record 
  or original receipt. No exceptions.\n\n
  
  ## Spare Parts & Calibre Inventory\n
  Spare parts for current production models are stocked at 
  the Shanghai and Tokyo CSCs. Legacy movements (discontinued 
  calibres >15 years) are managed via direct request to 
  Manufacture in Switzerland with a lead time of 
  **45–90 business days**.\n
  Boutique teams must proactively communicate legacy repair 
  timelines to clients at point of SAV intake — no 
  retrospective timeline surprises are acceptable.\n\n
  
  ## Client Communication Standards — SAV\n
  - SAV acknowledgement SMS/WeChat/LINE (market-dependent) 
  sent within **4 business hours** of item intake.\n
  - Milestone notifications: sent at intake, diagnosis 
  completion, repair completion, and dispatch.\n
  - If repair timeline is revised beyond original estimate, 
  boutique manager (not junior SA) must personally contact 
  the client within 24 hours of the delay being identified.\n\n
  
  ## SAV Revenue & Commercial Policy\n
  - SAV is not a primary revenue centre but contributes 
  approximately **4–6% of total boutique revenue** APAC-wide 
  when chargeable services (out-of-warranty repairs, 
  aesthetic restorations) are included.\n
  - Service pricing follows the **APAC SAV Tariff Grid v2025**, 
  issued annually by Supply Chain Operations. Market Directors 
  may not modify tariffs without Regional COO approval.\n
  - Out-of-warranty High Jewellery restorations above USD 5,000 
  require Regional Gemologist sign-off before client quotation 
  is issued.\n\n
  
  ## Quality Audit & Compliance\n
  - Each CSC undergoes a **bi-annual technical audit** conducted 
  by Manufacture's Quality team from Switzerland.\n
  - Mystery client SAV assessments are conducted **twice per year** 
  per market by the Client Experience team.\n
  - NPS (Net Promoter Score) for SAV interactions is tracked 
  separately from purchase NPS. APAC SAV NPS target: **72+**.

title: APAC Budget Planning, Revenue Reporting & Financial Governance
content:
Aurelle APAC — Budget Planning, Revenue Reporting & 
  Financial Governance Framework 2025\n\n
  
  ## Overview\n
  This document governs the annual budget planning cycle, 
  in-year revenue reporting standards, performance review 
  cadence, and financial governance protocols for all 
  Aurelle APAC markets. It is intended for Market Directors, 
  Finance Business Partners, and Regional VP Finance.\n\n
  
  ## APAC Financial Structure\n
  
  ### Revenue Breakdown by Category (2025 Budget)\n
  | Category             | Revenue Target (USD) | % of Total |\n
  |----------------------|----------------------|------------|\n
  | Watches              | 235.6M               | 38%        |\n
  | High Jewellery       | 136.4M               | 22%        |\n
  | Fine Jewellery       | 111.6M               | 18%        |\n
  | Leather & Accessories| 74.4M                | 12%        |\n
  | Fragrances & Beauty  | 37.2M                | 6%         |\n
  | Other (SAV, Bespoke) | 24.8M                | 4%         |\n
  | **TOTAL**            | **620M**             | **100%**   |\n\n
  
  ### Revenue Breakdown by Channel (2025 Budget)\n
  | Channel                   | % of Revenue | Target (USD) |\n
  |---------------------------|--------------|---------------|\n
  | Boutique (Retail)         | 60%          | 372M          |\n
  | Private Client Salon/VIC  | 18%          | 111.6M        |\n
  | Digital & Omnichannel     | 22%          | 136.4M        |\n\n
  
  ### Revenue Breakdown by Market (2025 Budget)\n
  | Market         | Revenue Target (USD) | YoY Growth Target |\n
  |----------------|----------------------|-------------------|\n
  | Mainland China | 198.4M               | +12%              |\n
  | Japan          | 99.2M                | +8%               |\n
  | Hong Kong      | 74.4M                | +5%               |\n
  | South Korea    | 68.2M                | +14%              |\n
  | Singapore      | 49.6M                | +10%              |\n
  | Australia      | 37.2M                | +6%               |\n
  | Taiwan         | 43.4M                | +7%               |\n
  | India          | 24.8M                | +18%              |\n
  | Thailand       | 12.4M                | +9%               |\n
  | Philippines    | 7.4M                 | +11%              |\n
  | Macau          | 5.0M                 | +3%               |\n
  | **TOTAL**      | **620M**             | **+10.2%**        |\n\n
  
  ## Annual Budget Planning Cycle\n
  
  ### Phase 1 — Strategic Frame (July–August, Year N-1)\n
  - Regional Finance VP and APAC President align with Paris HQ 
  on macro growth ambition and resource envelope.\n
  - Category and market growth assumptions validated against 
  luxury market forecasts (Bain & Company Luxury Study, 
  McKinsey China Luxury Report).\n
  - Preliminary APAC P&L framework issued to Market Directors 
  for input by **August 31**.\n\n
  
  ### Phase 2 — Bottom-Up Build (September–October, Year N-1)\n
  - Each market submits detailed bottom-up revenue and 
  cost budgets via the Aurelle Planning Portal (SAP BPC).\n
  - Inputs required per market: boutique-level revenue 
  phasing, headcount plan, capex requests (boutique 
  renovations, new openings), marketing investment plan, 
  and SAV revenue forecast.\n
  - Finance Business Partners conduct challenge sessions 
  with Market Directors during October.\n\n
  
  ### Phase 3 — Consolidation & Approval (November–December, Year N-1)\n
  - Regional Finance VP consolidates APAC budget and 
  presents to Paris CFO and APAC President.\n
  - Final budget approval received from Paris HQ 
  no later than **December 20**.\n
  - Approved budgets are locked in SAP BPC by **January 5** 
  of the operating year.\n\n
  
  ## In-Year Revenue Reporting Standards\n
  
  ### Reporting Cadence\n
  | Report            | Frequency  | Recipients                              |\n
  |-------------------|------------|------------------------------------------|\n
  | Flash Sales       | Daily      | Market Directors, Regional Sales VP       |\n
  | Weekly Sales Pack | Weekly     | APAC Leadership Team                     |\n
  | Monthly P&L       | Monthly    | APAC President, Finance VP, Paris CFO     |\n
  | Quarterly Review  | Quarterly  | Paris ExCo, APAC President               |\n
  | Annual Review     | Annual     | Global Board                              |\n\n
  
  ### Flash Sales Reporting Protocol\n
  - Daily flash is generated automatically from POS systems 
  (Oracle Retail) by **9:00 AM local market time**.\n
  - Flash reports include: daily net revenue by boutique, 
  category split, average transaction value (ATV), 
  number of transactions, and versus-budget variance.\n
  - Markets missing more than **3 daily flashes per month** 
  trigger an Operations Review with the Regional COO.\n\n
  
  ### Monthly P&L Structure\n
  Monthly P&Ls follow the Aurelle Global Chart of Accounts and 
  must include:\n
  - Net Revenue (after returns and staff sales)\n
  - Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) and Gross Margin %\n
  - Boutique Operating Costs (rent, staff, utilities)\n
  - Marketing & Events Investment\n
  - EBITDA and EBITDA margin %\n
  - Working Capital and Inventory Days\n\n
  APAC blended gross margin target: **68–72%** across categories.\n
  EBITDA margin target: **28–32%** at regional consolidated level.\n\n
  
  ## Capex & Investment Governance\n
  
  ### Capex Approval Thresholds\n
  | Investment Value     | Approval Authority              |\n
  |----------------------|---------------------------------|\n
  | < USD 50K            | Market Director                 |\n
  | USD 50K – 500K       | Regional COO + Finance VP       |\n
  | USD 500K – 2M        | APAC President                  |\n
  | > USD 2M             | Paris CFO + Global Real Estate  |\n\n
  
  ### Boutique Renovation & New Opening Capex Norms\n
  - **Flagship renovation** (>400 sqm): USD 2,500–4,000 per sqm.\n
  - **Standard boutique renovation** (<400 sqm): USD 1,800–2,500 per sqm.\n
  - **New boutique fit-out**: USD 3,000–5,000 per sqm 
  (market-dependent; Tokyo and Singapore command premium).\n
  - Renovation ROI threshold: payback within **5 years** 
  based on projected incremental revenue uplift.\n\n
  
  ## Staff Sales & Compliance\n
  - Aurelle staff are entitled to a **30% discount** on 
  personal purchases, capped at **USD 5,000 per year**.\n
  - Staff sales must be approved by the boutique director 
  and logged separately in POS under the 'Staff Sales' 
  channel code.\n
  - High Jewellery items are **excluded from staff sales 
  eligibility**.\n
  - Any misuse of staff sales privilege (third-party 
  purchasing, grey market resale) results in immediate 
  disciplinary review.\n\n
  
  ## Currency & FX Risk Management\n
  - All APAC market revenues are reported in **local currency** 
  at the point of sale and consolidated in **USD** at the 
  regional level using monthly average exchange rates 
  published by Paris Finance on the 1st of each month.\n
  - Budget FX rates are fixed at the start of the year 
  (January 1 rates, Paris Finance-published). 
  FX variance to budget is reported separately as 
  'FX Impact' in the monthly P&L and does not affect 
  market performance assessments.\n
  - Significant FX events (>5% movement in a market 
  currency within a month) trigger an ad hoc Finance 
  VP review and potential pricing review discussion 
  per the Pricing Architecture Policy (DOC-Finance-001).\n\n
  
  ## Audit & Financial Compliance\n
  - APAC markets are subject to **annual internal audit** 
  by Aurelle Global Internal Audit team.\n
  - Boutique cash reconciliation must be completed daily 
  by the boutique director or designated deputy.\n
  - Any variance >USD 500 in daily cash reconciliation 
  must be reported to Finance BP within 24 hours.\n
  - Aurelle's Anti-Corruption and Anti-Bribery policy 
  applies to all market operations — entertainment and 
  gifting to external parties must be pre-approved and 
  logged via the Compliance Portal.

title: APAC Learning & Development Framework — Client Advisor Excellence Program
content:
Aurelle APAC — Learning & Development Framework: 
  Client Advisor Excellence Program (CAEP)\n\n
  
  ## Program Overview\n
  The Client Advisor Excellence Program (CAEP) is Aurelle APAC's 
  comprehensive learning and development architecture for all 
  client-facing roles, from Sales Associates to Boutique 
  Directors. CAEP is designed to deliver measurable uplift 
  in three dimensions: **Product Mastery**, 
  **Client Experience Excellence**, and **Commercial Performance**.\n
  
  CAEP is administered by the APAC People Development team 
  in partnership with the global Maison Academy (Paris), 
  with market-level delivery coordinated by 
  Retail Training Managers in each key market.\n\n
  
  ## Target Audience & Role Mapping\n
  | Role                        | CAEP Track              | Priority Level |\n
  |-----------------------------|-------------------------|----------------|\n
  | New Sales Associate (<1yr)  | Foundation Track        | Mandatory      |\n
  | Experienced SA (1–3yrs)     | Advanced Client Track   | Mandatory      |\n
  | Senior SA / Team Leader     | Expert & Coaching Track | Mandatory      |\n
  | Boutique Manager            | Leadership & Commerce   | Mandatory      |\n
  | Boutique Director           | Strategic Leadership    | Mandatory      |\n
  | VIC / Personal Stylist      | VIC Mastery Track       | Mandatory      |\n
  | After-Sales Advisor         | SAV Excellence Track    | Mandatory      |\n\n
  
  ## CAEP Curriculum Architecture\n
  
  ### Track 1 — Foundation (0–6 Months, New Joiners)\n
  **Duration**: 120 hours structured learning over first 6 months.\n\n
  **Module 1 — Maison Heritage & Brand Codes** (16 hours)\n
  - Aurelle's 170-year history, founding philosophy, 
  and iconic Maison moments.\n
  - The 'Aurelle Aesthetic': core design codes 
  (geometric forms, panther motif, Trinity eternity).\n
  - Brand Positioning vs. competitive landscape: 
  how to articulate Aurelle's uniqueness authentically.\n
  - Assessment: Written brand narrative exercise, 
  scored by Retail Training Manager.\n\n
  **Module 2 — Product Knowledge Essentials** (32 hours)\n
  - Watches: Core collection mechanics, movement literacy, 
  key references (Santos, Tank, Ballon Bleu, Panthère), 
  materials, and water resistance standards.\n
  - Jewellery: Gemstone basics (4Cs of diamonds, 
  coloured stone grading), precious metal literacy 
  (platinum, yellow/rose/white gold), 
  hallmarking standards.\n
  - Fragrances: Olfactory family mapping, 
  key Aurelle fragrance signatures, 
  layering consultation basics.\n
  - Assessment: Product knowledge quiz (80% pass mark required). 
  Resit available once within 30 days.\n\n
  **Module 3 — Aurelle Selling Ceremony** (24 hours)\n
  - The 5-stage Aurelle Client Journey: 
  Welcome → Discovery → Presentation → Emotion → Conclusion.\n
  - Active listening and needs identification techniques.\n
  - Objection handling frameworks specific to luxury context 
  (price, availability, authenticity).\n
  - Role-play simulations: graded by Boutique Manager.\n\n
  **Module 4 — CRM & Client Data Protocols** (16 hours)\n
  - Client profile creation and data capture standards 
  (GDPR and APAC data privacy compliance — PDPA, PIPL).\n
  - CRM system navigation (Salesforce Luxury Edition).\n
  - How to read a client profile before an appointment: 
  CLV, purchase history, preferences, communication channel.\n
  - Escalation protocols for VIC client enquiries.\n\n
  **Module 5 — Boutique Operations Essentials** (32 hours)\n
  - POS system operation and transaction processing.\n
  - Inventory handling, stock reconciliation, 
  and security protocols.\n
  - SAV intake procedure and client communication standards.\n
  - Health, safety, and boutique security procedures.\n\n
  
  ### Track 2 — Advanced Client Excellence (6 Months – 3 Years)\n
  **Duration**: 80 hours per year (ongoing).\n\n
  **Module 1 — High Jewellery Mastery** (20 hours)\n
  - High Jewellery creation process: from concept atelier 
  to APAC private viewing.\n
  - Gemology deep-dive: exceptional stones, 
  provenance storytelling, GIA certification literacy.\n
  - High Jewellery client conversation frameworks: 
  building emotion, exclusivity, and legacy narratives.\n
  - Delivered by: Regional Gemologist (live) + 
  Maison Academy e-learning modules.\n\n
  **Module 2 — VIC Client Development** (20 hours)\n
  - Identifying and converting aspirational clients 
  to VIC status: CLV growth signals.\n
  - Personalization at scale: using CRM data to 
  design bespoke touchpoints.\n
  - Managing multi-year VIC relationships: 
  milestone moments, life event marketing.\n
  - Handling sensitive conversations: 
  pricing, exclusivity allocation, waitlists.\n\n
  **Module 3 — Watches Technical Excellence** (20 hours)\n
  - In-depth calibre knowledge: 
  mechanical vs. automatic vs. quartz movements.\n
  - Complications literacy: tourbillon, perpetual calendar, 
  minute repeater — client-appropriate explanation techniques.\n
  - Watch investment conversation: 
  how to position fine watchmaking as heritage and value.\n
  - Market-specific watch demand: Japan's collector culture, 
  Korea's Santos positioning, China's complication appetite.\n\n
  **Module 4 — Commercial Excellence** (20 hours)\n
  - Conversion rate optimization techniques.\n
  - Average Transaction Value (ATV) uplift strategies: 
  cross-category selling, gifting narratives.\n
  - Understanding boutique P&L basics: 
  how individual SA performance connects to boutique EBITDA.\n
  - Upselling and upgrading within the Aurelle ecosystem 
  without compromising client trust.\n\n
  
  ### Track 3 — VIC Mastery (Personal Stylists & Senior SAs)\n
  **Duration**: 60 hours per year + 1 Paris Maison immersion.\n\n
  - **Paris Maison Immersion** (5 days): 
  Atelier visits (jewellery and watchmaking), 
  gemstone sourcing presentation, 
  archive collections viewing, 
  executive leadership presentations.\n
  - **Bespoke & Personalisation Masterclass**: 
  Bespoke jewellery design process, 
  client co-creation facilitation, 
  managing expectations on timeline and pricing.\n
  - **Elite Hosting & Events Protocol**: 
  Private salon event management, 
  champagne and catering standards, 
  protocol for international VIC guests.\n
  - **Digital Intimacy**: Using WeChat, LINE, KakaoTalk 
  for hyper-personalized VIC engagement — 
  tone, frequency, content boundaries.\n\n
  
  ### Track 4 — Leadership & Boutique Commerce 
  (Boutique Managers & Directors)\n
  **Duration**: 40 hours per year + quarterly leadership forums.\n\n
  - **P&L Management for Retail Leaders**: 
  Reading boutique financials, managing cost lines, 
  connecting team performance to commercial outcomes.\n
  - **People Leadership in Luxury**: 
  Coaching and developing SA talent, 
  managing performance conversations, 
  retaining top client advisors.\n
  - **Strategic Client Portfolio Management**: 
  Boutique-level VIC strategy, 
  event planning and ROI assessment, 
  pipeline management for High Jewellery.\n
  - **Change Leadership**: 
  Implementing new systems, collection launches, 
  and boutique renovations with minimal client disruption.\n\n
  
  ## Learning Delivery Methods\n
  | Method                        | % of Total Hours | Notes                            |\n
  |-------------------------------|-----------------|----------------------------------|\n
  | In-boutique coaching          | 35%             | Daily manager observation        |\n
  | Maison Academy e-learning     | 30%             | Self-paced, mobile-first platform|\n
  | Live workshops (regional)     | 20%             | Quarterly per market             |\n
  | Paris Maison immersion        | 10%             | VIC Track and Directors only     |\n
  | External (gemology, retail)   | 5%              | GIA courses, Luxury Institute    |\n\n
  
  ## Assessment & Certification Standards\n
  - All modules carry a **minimum pass mark of 80%**.\n
  - Certifications are valid for **2 years**, 
  after which recertification is required.\n
  - CAEP certification status is visible on employee 
  profiles in the HR system (Workday) and 
  considered in promotion eligibility assessments.\n
  - Market Training Managers report CAEP completion 
  rates monthly to the Regional People Development VP.\n
  - APAC-wide CAEP completion rate target: **90%** 
  of eligible employees certified at their track level.\n\n
  
  ## Performance Link — CAEP to Commercial KPIs\n
  CAEP effectiveness is measured via direct commercial 
  outcome tracking:\n
  - **ATV progression**: SAs completing Advanced Track 
  show average 18% ATV improvement within 6 months 
  (2024 APAC benchmark).\n
  - **Conversion rate**: Foundation Track completions 
  correlate with 12% improvement in boutique 
  conversion rates vs. non-certified peers.\n
  - **Client retention**: VIC Track SAs manage client 
  portfolios with 22% higher repeat purchase rates.\n\n
  These metrics are reviewed in the **Annual Talent 
  & Commerce Review** held each February, co-chaired 
  by the Regional VP People and Regional VP Sales.

title: Aurelle APAC Brand Strategy & Competitive Positioning 2025
content:
Aurelle APAC Brand Strategy & Competitive Positioning 2025\n\n
  
  ## Strategic Vision\n
  Aurelle's APAC brand strategy for 2025 operates under the global mandate 
  'Timeless Modernity' — preserving Maison heritage while accelerating 
  relevance across new-wealth demographics and digitally native luxury 
  consumers. APAC contributes 38% of Aurelle's global revenue, positioning 
  the region as the single most critical growth engine for the Maison 
  worldwide.\n\n
  
  ## Brand Positioning Framework\n
  Aurelle competes in the ultra-premium and high jewellery tier, 
  differentiated by three core pillars:\n
  1. **Savoir-Faire Authenticity**: All High Jewellery and Watchmaking 
  narratives anchor on Aurelle's 170-year Maison heritage, Parisian 
  ateliers, and hand-craftsmanship traditions. Regional communication 
  must never dilute this narrative.\n
  2. **Aspirational Accessibility**: Entry-level categories (Trinity 
  collection, Fragrances, and Santos de Aurelle entry watches) serve 
  as the client acquisition funnel, enabling aspirational consumers 
  to enter the Maison ecosystem before graduating to higher-value 
  categories.\n
  3. **Radical Exclusivity at the Top**: High Jewellery and 
  Ultra-High Complications remain deliberately scarce, reinforcing 
  perceived and intrinsic value at the Maison's apex.\n\n
  
  ## Competitive Landscape — APAC 2025\n
  Aurelle's primary competitive set in APAC comprises:\n
  - **Tier 1 Rivals** (direct competition, full category overlap): 
  Van Cleef & Arpels, Bvlgari, Chopard, Harry Winston.\n
  - **Tier 2 Rivals** (watches and accessories overlap): Rolex, 
  Patek Philippe, IWC, Jaeger-LeCoultre.\n
  - **Emerging Threat**: Chinese domestic luxury brands (Chow Tai 
  Fook Jewellery's high-end sub-brands, NE·TIGER) showing growing 
  traction in Tier 2 Chinese cities. These are monitored quarterly 
  by the Regional Strategy team but do not yet compete at the 
  High Jewellery level.\n\n
  Aurelle's differentiation advantage is the **Trinity-to-Haute-Joaillerie 
  continuum** — no direct competitor offers the same breadth of product 
  from entry-level gifting to USD 5M+ bespoke creations under a single 
  coherent Maison narrative.\n\n
  
  ## Market-Specific Brand Priorities\n
  
  ### Greater China (Mainland China, Hong Kong, Macau)\n
  - **Priority**: Volume leadership and VIC cultivation. China 
  remains the highest absolute revenue contributor within APAC.\n
  - **Brand Narrative**: Emphasize Maison heritage storytelling 
  through WeChat Channels, RED (Xiaohongshu), and Douyin short-form 
  content. Localize storytelling without altering brand codes.\n
  - **Key Risk**: Grey market price arbitrage remains a structural 
  threat, particularly for LOVE Bracelet and Santos segments. 
  Pricing harmonization protocols (see DOC-Finance) are critical.\n
  - **2025 Priority Cities**: Shanghai, Beijing, Chengdu, Shenzhen, 
  Hangzhou (digital activation only).\n\n
  
  ### Japan\n
  - **Priority**: Margin quality over volume. Japanese clientele 
  demonstrate the highest product knowledge and brand sensitivity 
  in APAC. Quality of service and heritage articulation are 
  non-negotiable.\n
  - **Brand Narrative**: Craftsmanship, timelessness, and 
  understated prestige resonate most strongly.\n
  - **2025 Priority**: Expand Watches category share — Santos de 
  Aurelle and Ballon Bleu — in response to resurgent local 
  watch demand and inbound tourist spending at Ginza boutique.\n\n
  
  ### South Korea\n
  - **Priority**: Younger affluent demographics (25–38 age bracket). 
  Korea leads APAC in influencer-driven luxury discovery; 
  Aurelle must balance exclusivity narrative with targeted 
  digital visibility.\n
  - **Brand Narrative**: Couple gifting rituals (LOVE, Trinity) 
  continue to anchor Korean market activations. High Jewellery 
  awareness is growing through K-celebrity ambassador alignment.\n
  - **2025 Priority**: Cheongdam boutique renovation completion 
  and hosting of first Seoul High Jewellery Gala event.\n\n
  
  ### Southeast Asia (Singapore, Thailand, Philippines)\n
  - **Priority**: VIC network deepening. SEA has a smaller but 
  highly concentrated ultra-high-net-worth client base. 
  Private salon events outperform boutique walk-in traffic 
  by a ratio of 3:1 in revenue terms.\n
  - **Brand Narrative**: Heritage + global prestige signaling. 
  SEA clients are highly travel-retail aware and compare 
  Paris/London/Dubai pricing. Consistent cross-market pricing 
  is critical.\n
  - **2025 Priority**: Singapore as APAC's regional events hub 
  for High Jewellery, anchored at ION Orchard Maison Mansion.\n\n
  
  ### India\n
  - **Priority**: Long-term market development. India is classified 
  as an 'Investment Market' in 2025 — boutique network is limited 
  to Delhi (DLF Emporio) and Mumbai (Bandra Kurla Complex). 
  Revenue targets are below APAC average but brand visibility 
  KPIs are elevated.\n
  - **Brand Narrative**: Wedding jewellery positioning is uniquely 
  powerful in India. Bespoke High Jewellery services and 
  bridal customization programs are the primary revenue driver.\n
  - **2025 Priority**: Introduce private client bridal consultation 
  program anchored in both boutiques. Expand CLV tracking 
  for NRI (Non-Resident Indian) clients shopping in Singapore, 
  HK, and Dubai.\n\n
  
  ### Australia & Taiwan\n
  - **Priority**: Steady-state premium market management. Both 
  markets demonstrate stable, repeat-driven client bases with 
  lower new-client acquisition velocity.\n
  - **Australia**: Sydney (Westfield Sydney) and Melbourne 
  (Collins Street) boutiques serve a high proportion of 
  Chinese-Australian affluent clientele. Communications 
  should be bilingual where CRM data supports.\n
  - **Taiwan**: Taipei 101 boutique serves as a high-visibility 
  flagship. Taiwan clients are among the most loyal in APAC 
  with above-average CLV tenure.\n\n
  
  ## Key Strategic Bets 2025\n
  1. **VIC Concentration Strategy**: Shift 15% of total marketing 
  budget from mass-digital to hyper-personalized VIC experiences 
  (private events, bespoke ateliers, Maison visits to Paris).\n
  2. **Watches Category Acceleration**: Capture growing 
  'investment watch' sentiment in Japan and Korea — position 
  Aurelle Complications as both cultural and financial assets.\n
  3. **Omnichannel Seamlessness**: By Q3 2025, all APAC markets 
  must offer a unified client profile viewable by stylists 
  in-boutique and CRM teams simultaneously, enabling 
  real-time personalization at point of sale.\n
  4. **Sustainability Narrative Integration**: 
  Aurelle's 'Responsible Sourcing Charter' (ethically sourced 
  diamonds, recycled precious metals) must be woven into all 
  High Jewellery and Fine Watchmaking client conversations — 
  mandatory from Q2 2025 per global Maison directive.\n\n
  
  ## Brand Governance & Compliance\n
  - All regional campaigns must receive validation from 
  Paris HQ Brand Direction team minimum 45 days pre-launch.\n
  - Local adaptations (language, talent casting, event formats) 
  are permitted within 'APAC Brand Expression Guidelines v3.2' 
  parameters.\n
  - Any deviation from Maison visual identity standards triggers 
  an automatic escalation to Regional VP Communications.

title: Aurelle APAC Sales Strategy 2025
content:
Aurelle APAC Sales Strategy 2025 — Executive Summary

STRATEGIC CONTEXT
The APAC region represents Aurelle's single largest growth engine globally, targeting USD 620M in net revenue for FY2025, representing a +14% YoY growth versus FY2024 actuals of USD 543M. APAC contributes 34% of global Aurelle revenue, second only to Europe (38%), with the gap narrowing by approximately 2 percentage points annually. The strategy is anchored on three convictions: (1) physical retail excellence remains non-negotiable in luxury APAC, (2) digital must amplify — not replace — the human relationship, and (3) the watches category will define Aurelle's identity leadership in the region.

MARKET REVENUE TARGETS FY2025
- Greater China (Mainland + HK + Macau): USD 310M (50% of APAC total)
- Japan: USD 85M (14%)
- South Korea: USD 62M (10%)
- Southeast Asia cluster (SG, TH, PH, MY): USD 93M (15%)
- Australia + India: USD 42M (7%)
- Taiwan: USD 28M (4%)

GROWTH PILLAR 1 — CHINA PHYSICAL EXPANSION
Mainland China boutique network to grow from 22 to 27 doors by Q3 2025. Priority openings: Chengdu Taikoo Li (Q1 — flagship, 420 sqm), Shenzhen MixC (Q2 — standard, 280 sqm), Nanjing Deji (Q3 — standard, 260 sqm). Existing doors in Tier-1 cities (Shanghai Plaza 66, Beijing SKP, Guangzhou IFS) to undergo full renovation cycles with enhanced VIC salon spaces. Rationale: Domestic luxury consumption remains elevated as outbound tourism recovery is partial. Chengdu and Shenzhen capture the emerging HNWI population under 40 in Tier-2 cities. Tourist recovery corridor (HK–Macau–Guangzhou) expected to recover to 78% of pre-COVID levels by mid-2025.

GROWTH PILLAR 2 — WATCHES CATEGORY LEADERSHIP
Watches represent 38% of APAC revenue (USD 236M target) and are the primary acquisition category for new clients under 35. Anchor references: Santos de Aurelle (top revenue volume, entry and high complications), Tank Must (highest volume units, entry luxury), Ballon Bleu (strongest female client base, highest cross-sell to jewellery). Strategy includes: dedicated watch ambassador program across 8 flagship boutiques, partnership with Watches & Wonders Geneva 2025 for APAC media activation, and a watches-only private event calendar (minimum 24 events APAC-wide in 2025).

GROWTH PILLAR 3 — OMNICHANNEL DIGITALIZATION
Digital-assisted revenue (where digital touchpoint preceded purchase) to scale from 15% (FY2024) to 22% of total revenue (FY2025). Key platforms: WeChat Mini-Program (China, 8M monthly active followers), LINE CRM automation (Japan, Thailand), KakaoTalk (South Korea), and a newly launched Aurelle APAC Client App (iOS/Android, rolling out Q1 2025, SG/AU/HK first). Digital-physical integration mandates that any client interaction digitally initiated must be assigned to their designated stylist within 4 business hours. Digital revenue does not replace boutique relationship — all high-value transactions above USD 15,000 must be completed in-person or via secure video consultation with stylist present.

BOUTIQUE NETWORK PERFORMANCE
Boutique network = 60% of total APAC revenue. Average boutique revenue: USD 8.2M/year (range: USD 2.1M regional to USD 38M flagship). Private Client Salon viewings held in Maison Mansions remain highest-margin channel at 76.8% gross margin, driven by High Jewellery, High Complications watches, and bespoke services. Salons also demonstrate superior retention: clients who have attended a Maison event show 82% repeat purchase rate within 18 months vs. 54% boutique average.

TARGET KPIs FY2025
- Total APAC Revenue: USD 620M
- Repeat Client Rate: 65% APAC-wide (vs. 59% FY2024)
- New Client Acquisition: 48,000 clients (first purchase)
- VIC Client Base Growth (Tier 1, CLV >USD 50K): +18% YoY
- NPS Score: 72 (vs. 68 FY2024)
- Digital-Assisted Revenue: 22% of total
- High Jewellery Revenue: USD 74M (12% of APAC total)
- Employee Turnover (client-facing): <12%

COMPETITIVE CONTEXT
Primary competitors in APAC: Van Cleef & Arpels (jewellery share), Rolex/Patek Philippe (watches, non-overlapping positioning but wallet-of-client competition), Bulgari (jewellery + watches overlap), Tiffany & Co post-LVMH repositioning (direct threat, accelerating APAC). Aurelle's differentiation: heritage narrative depth (est. 1847 equivalent), breadth of offering (watches + jewellery + accessories + fragrance), and the strength of iconic codes (LOVE, Trinity, Panthère, Santos) that are immediately recognizable without logo dependency.

title: Aurelle Product Universe — Fragrance & Accessories Portfolio APAC
content:
Aurelle Fragrance & Accessories Portfolio — APAC Commercial Reference

STRATEGIC ROLE IN PORTFOLIO
Fragrance and accessories represent approximately 14% of APAC revenue but are disproportionately important for two strategic reasons: (1) Client acquisition — they are the most accessible entry point into the Aurelle universe and frequently precede a first jewellery or watch purchase within 12–24 months; (2) Gifting completeness — they allow clients to give Aurelle as a gift at any occasion and any budget level, keeping the brand present across life events.

FRAGRANCE PORTFOLIO

LA PANTHÈRE EDP (FLAGSHIP)
The Maison's iconic feminine fragrance. Green floral-chypre profile: chestnut flower accord, gardenia, white musk. The panther claw bottle design is immediately recognizable. Available in 50ml (~USD 230) and 75ml (~USD 310).
APAC Role: Highest volume fragrance SKU. Particularly strong gifting in China (Golden Week, Valentine's Day, CNY), Japan (gift culture extraordinaire), and Korea.
Selling Narrative: 'The panthere does not announce herself — she arrives in a cloud of gardenia and green air. La Panthère is the first jewel you cannot see but everyone can sense.'

LES HEURES DE AURELLE (NICHE LINE)
A collection of 18 unique fragrances, each representing an hour of the Parisian day. Positioned as ultra-premium niche parfumerie. Prices from USD 380–USD 850 per 75ml.
Target: Fragrance connoisseurs, Tier 1–2 clients, clients seeking a non-obvious luxury gift for a sophisticated recipient.
APAC Role: Strong in Japan (perfume culture depth), Singapore (sophisticated multicultural client base), and growing in Korea. Lower penetration in China where occidental fragrance education is still developing.

DÉCLARATION (MASCULINE)
Aurelle's primary masculine fragrance. Woody spicy oriental: cardamom, cedar, vetiver. Seen as the 'gentleman's fragrance' with strong recognition in traditional markets.
Key markets: Japan (strong), India (growing, masculine fragrance gifting culture strong), Australia.

ACCESSORIES PORTFOLIO

ÉCROU DE AURELLE COLLECTION
Small leather goods — card holders, wallets, passport holders — featuring the screw motif (same code as LOVE and Juste un Clou). Entry price: USD 340 (card holder). Strong acquisition piece for Tier 3 and prospect clients.
Cross-sell: Strong bridge to LOVE bracelet — shared design code makes the narrative easy: 'You already carry the screw every day — now wear it.'

WATCH STRAPS & BRACELETS
Interchangeable straps for Santos and select references. Alligator, rubber, and fabric options from USD 280–USD 1,200. Strong add-on purchase for existing watch clients — stylists should suggest a second strap at every watch purchase and at service appointments.

SUNGLASSES & FINE ACCESSORIES
Selected eyewear (Panthère frames, Santos-inspired rectangular frames) and scarves. Seasonal items. Primarily boutique gift shop equivalent, though quality is premium. Prices USD 380–USD 980.

GIFTING CALENDAR FOR ACCESSORIES/FRAGRANCE
- CNY (Jan/Feb): La Panthère gift sets, LOVE pendant + fragrance combo sets — pre-pack for corporate gifting
- Valentine's Day (Feb 14): His-and-hers fragrance duo, LOVE ring + La Panthère set
- White Day (Mar 14, JP/KR/TW): Fragrance and small leather goods — male-to-female gift culture
- Mother's Day (May, market-specific): Trinity + La Panthère — feminine, multi-generational narrative
- June (Graduation season in JP): Entry watches and fragrance — younger client acquisition
- Diwali (Oct/Nov, IN): Gold jewellery and fragrance gifting — Diwali box sets should be developed specifically for Indian market
- Christmas/New Year: Largest gifting period globally — all fragrance SKUs offered in special packaging

title: Aurelle Product Universe — Watches Portfolio APAC
content:
Aurelle Watches Portfolio — APAC Sales & Product Knowledge Reference

PORTFOLIO OVERVIEW
Aurelle's watch collections represent 38% of APAC revenue and serve as the primary acquisition vehicle for clients under 40. The portfolio spans from entry luxury (Tank Must, ~USD 3,200) to Grand Complications (Rotonde Ultra-Thin Perpetual Calendar, ~USD 98,000+), offering a complete journey from first purchase to ultimate horological expression. Aurelle occupies the position of the 'architect's watch' — design-led, geometrically distinctive, heritage-rich but never anachronistic.

COLLECTION PROFILES

SANTOS DE AURELLE
Heritage: Designed 1904 for Alberto Santos-Dumont, the aviation pioneer — the first wristwatch made for a man of action. One of the most copied designs in history, which reinforces original ownership narrative.
Design DNA: Square case with exposed screws on bezel and bracelet — the signature 'vis' detail. Available in steel, SSGP (Aurelle's proprietary steel-gold blend), bicolor, and full gold. Interchangeable strap system (Quiklage) is a key client engagement feature — stylists should demonstrate in boutique.
APAC Commercial Role: Top revenue-generating watch collection. Primary target: male clients 28–45, professional sector, first serious watch purchase or step-up from fashion watches. Strongest markets: China (aspirational professional), Korea (style-driven), Australia (entry luxury male demographic).
Key References:
- Santos Medium Steel: ~USD 7,100 — highest volume SKU in APAC
- Santos Large SSGP: ~USD 8,500 — most popular bicolor, strong in KR and CN
- Santos de Aurelle Skeleton: ~USD 19,500 — high visibility piece, boutique display anchor
- Santos 100 Chronograph: ~USD 14,200 — motorsport/aviation client appeal
Selling Narrative: 'The Santos was the world's first functional wristwatch — not made for fashion, but for flight. Every Santos client today inherits that spirit of purposeful innovation.'
Cross-sell: After Santos acquisition, highest cross-sell success to LOVE bracelet (gifting for partner) or second Santos in different material.

TANK MUST
Heritage: The Tank was born in 1917, inspired by the aerial view of military tanks on the Western Front. Has been worn by every icon from Jackie Kennedy to Andy Warhol to Princess Diana.
Design DNA: Pure rectangular geometry, Roman numeral dial, cabochon crown — the most copied watch silhouette in history. Tank Must is the heritage-accessible entry: quartz movement, lacquered dials in color options, SolarBeat™ photovoltaic movement (sustainable positioning).
APAC Commercial Role: Highest unit volume watch in APAC. Primary target: female clients 25–45, second watch (complement to sports/casual watch already owned), gift purchase. Also strong with male clients seeking a dress watch.
Key References:
- Tank Must Small Quartz (lacquered colors): ~USD 3,200 — entry luxury, highest gifting volume
- Tank Must Large SolarBeat™: ~USD 4,100 — sustainability messaging resonates in AU, SG, JP
- Tank Louis (higher heritage tier): ~USD 7,800 — for clients seeking the 'pure' Tank expression
- Tank Cintrée (limited, elongated vintage form): ~USD 12,500 — highest desirability, waitlist in JP and TW
Selling Narrative: 'The Tank has been on the wrists of presidents, artists, and icons for over a century — not because of what it says about wealth, but because of what it says about taste.'
Cross-sell: Tank → Trinity ring (shared geometric heritage narrative). Tank → La Panthère watch (for clients seeking something more expressive).

BALLON BLEU DE AURELLE
Heritage: Launched 2007 — Aurelle's most commercially successful modern creation. Named for the blue sapphire cabochon protecting the winding crown, an innovation that became a new luxury signifier.
Design DNA: Round case with softly curved lugs, signature blue cabochon crown concealed in the case arch — feminine yet substantial. Available in 33mm, 36mm, and 42mm — true unisex range. Steel, bicolor, full gold, and diamond-set versions.
APAC Commercial Role: Female-dominant purchase (72% female buyers APAC). Strongest emotional connection of any watch in the portfolio. Highest cross-category collector depth: clients who buy Ballon Bleu often also purchase La Panthère or High Jewellery.
Key References:
- Ballon Bleu 33mm Steel: ~USD 6,800 — entry, highest female volume
- Ballon Bleu 36mm Bicolor Diamond Bezel: ~USD 11,500 — highest revenue per unit in collection
- Ballon Bleu 42mm Full Gold: ~USD 28,000 — male buyer, wealth signal
- Ballon Bleu Tourbillon: ~USD 72,000 — top of range, HJ client crossover
Selling Narrative: 'The blue cabochon is not a stone chosen for decoration — it is a functional guardian of the movement, a touch of poetry in a world of precision.'
Cross-sell: Ballon Bleu 33mm → Ballon Bleu 36mm upgrade within 24 months (highest same-collection repeat purchase rate: 34%). Also cross-sells strongly to LOVE bracelet and Trinity.

LA PANTHÈRE DE AURELLE
Heritage: The Panthère emblem has been Aurelle's most iconic motif since the 1910s — adopted as the totem of the Maison, personifying elegance, power, and sensuality.
Design DNA: Square-in-round case (the 'panther eye' aperture), hidden in a bracelet that IS the watch — blurring jewelry and timepiece. Full gold or bicolor with onyx and diamond pavé details.
APAC Commercial Role: Highest average transaction value watch after complications. Target: female clients, Tier 1–2, fashion and jewelry-forward. Strong in Japan (aesthetic refinement culture), Korea (brand status), and HK.
Key References:
- Panthère Small Steel: ~USD 5,500 — entry jewelry-watch
- Panthère Medium Full Gold Diamond Bracelet: ~USD 34,000 — top Tier 2 / Tier 1 cross-sell
- Panthère Tourbillon: ~USD 115,000 — High Jewellery territory, allocated individually
Selling Narrative: 'The Panthère does not tell time — it commands it. Every client who wears it joins a century of Aurelle women who never followed, only led.'

ROTONDE DE AURELLE — COMPLICATIONS
Heritage: Aurelle's horological pinnacle — showcasing the Manufacture's mastery of grand complications including tourbillon, perpetual calendar, minute repeater, and celestial charts.
APAC Commercial Role: Low volume, very high value. Primary audience: serious watch collectors, Tier 1 Icon clients. Japan is by far the strongest market (collector culture). Also growing in China among second-generation wealth inheritors with horological interest.
Key References:
- Rotonde Ultra-Thin Perpetual Calendar: ~USD 98,000
- Rotonde Tourbillon: ~USD 165,000
- Rotonde Minute Repeater Flying Tourbillon: ~USD 410,000 — APAC allocation: 4 pieces annually
Selling Notes: Requires specialized Watch Specialist (not standard stylist) to present. Client knowledge should be gauged before technical depth — some clients are experts, others are buying the emotional narrative of rarity. Do not over-explain to the former; do not under-explain to the latter.

ULYSSE — SPORT & CONTEMPORARY (NEW FY2025)
New collection launching Q2 2025. Aurelle's answer to the sport-luxury segment (competing with AP Royal Oak adjacency in positioning). Target: male clients 30–45, HNWI with active lifestyle. Steel and titanium. Integrated bracelet. Swiss manufacture automatic movement.
Initial references: 40mm and 44mm. Pricing: USD 12,500–USD 19,000.
Strategic rationale: Recapture clients currently choosing sport-luxury alternatives while staying in Aurelle relationship. Pre-launch waitlist to be managed via CRM — priority to Tier 1 Santos and Ballon Bleu collectors.

GENERAL WATCH SELLING PRINCIPLES FOR APAC
1. Always begin with the heritage story — price follows meaning, not the reverse
2. Demonstrate the physical object: Quiklage strap change for Santos, sapphire cabochon for Ballon Bleu, the bracelet transformation for Panthère
3. Market-specific sensitivity: Japan clients value movement quality and restraint; Korean clients value recognizability and lifestyle imagery; Chinese clients value heritage narrative and investment narrative; Australian clients value practicality and design story
4. Never position Aurelle watches against competitors directly — elevate to Aurelle's own universe
5. The 'watch of a lifetime' narrative is valid for complications; the 'signature of style' narrative for Tank and Panthère; the 'tool for ambition' narrative for Santos

title: Aurelle Product Universe — Jewellery Portfolio APAC
content:
Aurelle Jewellery Portfolio — APAC Sales & Product Knowledge Reference

PORTFOLIO OVERVIEW
Aurelle's jewellery portfolio is the Maison's founding purpose and deepest expression of identity. The portfolio spans four commercial tiers: iconic (LOVE, Trinity, Juste un Clou, Panthère motif), fine jewellery (Diamants Légers, Écrou de Aurelle, new seasonal collections), bridal & solitaires, and High Jewellery (unique or limited pieces in precious stones). Jewellery represents 48% of APAC revenue, with the iconic tier alone contributing 31% of total Aurelle APAC revenue — the single largest revenue cluster.

ICONIC COLLECTIONS — DETAILED PROFILES

LOVE COLLECTION
Heritage: Created in 1969 by Aldo Cipullo for the NYC clientele — a bracelet that could only be removed with a special screwdriver, a physical metaphor for love that cannot be undone. Worn by Elizabeth Taylor, Sophia Loren, and generations of devoted couples since.
Design DNA: Oval screw-motif bracelet in 18K gold (yellow, white, rose), set with screwheads as the only decoration. Available with and without diamonds. Extends to rings, earrings, pendants, and necklaces maintaining the screw-head motif.
APAC Commercial Role: Highest volume jewellery collection in APAC by units. The #1 gifting piece at Aurelle — Valentine's Day, anniversaries, Christmas, CNY. Also significant self-purchase by female clients who 'give themselves the LOVE.' The bracelet is a cultural signal in APAC: immediately recognized and aspirationally positioned.
Key References:
- LOVE Bracelet Yellow Gold (no stones): ~USD 6,750 — highest volume SKU in entire jewellery portfolio
- LOVE Bracelet Yellow Gold Full Diamonds: ~USD 21,500 — premium gifting, anniversaries
- LOVE Ring YG: ~USD 1,300 — highest volume entry, accessible aspirational
- LOVE Earrings (small screw): ~USD 2,100 — strong female self-purchase
- LOVE Necklace (small pendant): ~USD 1,850 — entry client acquisition
Gifting Selling Narrative: 'The LOVE bracelet cannot be removed without the screwdriver — a deliberate choice. It is not jewelry you wear sometimes. It is a commitment you wear always.'
Self-Purchase Narrative: 'The best love story you can tell starts with the love you have for yourself.'
Market Notes: In China, the LOVE bracelet is culturally linked to milestone gifting — 520 (Chinese Valentine's equivalent, May 20th) and 7/7 (Qixi Festival) generate 40% of annual LOVE bracelet revenue in 6 weeks. In Korea, LOVE ring is the #1 couple's ring at Aurelle. In Japan, the LOVE bangle (wider version) is gaining traction as a self-purchase luxury statement for working women. In Australia, LOVE is primarily Valentine's and anniversary — less self-purchase culture vs. Asia.
Cross-sell: LOVE bracelet YG → LOVE bracelet WG or RG as second or third unit (significant 'stacking' culture, especially in SG, HK, and CN). → Ballon Bleu watch as a pair. → High Jewellery once client relationship matures.

TRINITY COLLECTION
Heritage: Three interlocking bands of yellow, white, and rose gold, created in 1924. Jean Cocteau, Yves Saint Laurent, and countless cultural figures have worn it. The three golds traditionally represent fidelity, friendship, and love.
Design DNA: Three smooth bands, interlocked but free to move slightly — suggesting connection without constraint. The simplicity is deceptive; the tricolor execution requires extraordinary goldsmithing precision. Available as rings, bracelets, necklaces, earrings.
APAC Commercial Role: Second highest volume jewellery collection. Primary acquisition piece for Tier 3 clients — the first Aurelle jewellery many clients own. Entry price point makes it accessible; the heritage and recognizability make it prestigious. Equally popular male and female.
Key References:
- Trinity Ring Classic (3 bands, small): ~USD 980 — #1 Tier 3 acquisition piece
- Trinity Ring Large: ~USD 1,750 — upgrade from classic, strong in KR and CN
- Trinity Bracelet: ~USD 4,200 — step-up for Tier 2 clients
- Trinity Necklace: ~USD 3,100 — strong female self-purchase, Japan market
- Trinity Earrings: ~USD 2,400 — strong gifting
Selling Narrative: 'The three colors have always meant something different to every person who wears them — that ambiguity is intentional. Aurelle does not prescribe love; it celebrates its infinite forms.'
Market Notes: In Taiwan and Philippines, Trinity ring is the dominant entry piece — cultural preference for subtle, quality-first luxury over logo-driven pieces aligns with Trinity's understated design. In India, Trinity has grown 60% in FY2024 — the tricolor gold resonates with gold-appreciation culture while offering international modernity.
Cross-sell: Trinity Ring → LOVE bracelet (complementary size/weight balance). Trinity → Santos watch (shared geometric heritage — 'right angle meets triple circle' narrative). Trinity → Juste un Clou (styling contrast).

JUSTE UN CLOU
Heritage: Created 1971, also by Aldo Cipullo — a nail, bent into a bracelet. The ultimate triumph of conceptual art over conventional jewelry design. Initially considered provocative; now recognized as a landmark of 20th-century design.
Design DNA: A nail — the hardware store object — in 18K gold or platinum, set with diamonds. Available as bracelets, rings, earrings, and necklaces. The tension between the mundane object and precious material creates the conversation.
APAC Commercial Role: High-margin, strong with fashion-forward and culturally aware clients. Not the highest volume but commands above-average transaction values. Strong with creative industry clients (fashion, media, tech) who appreciate the conceptual backstory.
Key References:
- Juste un Clou Bracelet YG: ~USD 7,500
- Juste un Clou Ring WG Diamonds: ~USD 4,800
- Juste un Clou Earrings (nail motif): ~USD 3,200
Selling Narrative: 'When Cipullo bent a nail into gold, critics thought he was joking. Over fifty years later, it is still the most provocative bracelet Aurelle has ever made — because it asks: what is luxury if not a radical reimagining of the ordinary?'
Market Notes: Particularly strong in Tokyo (design culture), Seoul (fashion-forward young luxury), and Singapore (internationally educated creative class). Less intuitive in markets with stronger traditional jewelry preferences (India, some Mainland China tiers).

PANTHÈRE MOTIF JEWELLERY
Heritage: The panther has prowled through Aurelle's creative universe since 1914. Jeanne Toussaint — Aurelle's legendary creative director nicknamed 'La Panthère' — made it the Maison's totem animal.
Design DNA: Three-dimensional panther figures in white gold, yellow gold, and platinum, pavé-set with diamonds and black onyx spots, emerald eyes. Ranges from playful small pendants to fully sculptural High Jewellery brooches and necklaces.
APAC Commercial Role: Mid-to-high price point. Primarily Tier 1–2 clients. High emotional resonance — the panther is a statement about the wearer's character, not just their wealth. Strongest in Japan and Hong Kong.
Key References:
- Panthère de Aurelle Ring (onyx, emerald): ~USD 5,200
- Panthère Pendant Small WG Diamonds: ~USD 8,400
- Panthère Bracelet Full Pavé: ~USD 44,000 — Tier 1 territory
Selling Narrative: 'The panther has always been Aurelle's most personal symbol — not a heraldic crest, but a living character. Every woman who wears the Panthère chooses to embody what it represents: intelligence, grace, and the willingness to surprise.'

BRIDAL & SOLITAIRE
Aurelle's bridal offering ranges from classic solitaires to signature collections. The Destinée and 1895 collections are primary APAC bridal references.
- 1895 Solitaire (round brilliant, platinum): from USD 8,500 (0.5ct) to USD 85,000+ (3ct+)
- Destinée (innovative prong setting, diamond appears suspended): from USD 7,200
Market notes: Bridal is strongest in Japan (highest per-capita engagement ring spend globally, Aurelle competes directly with local and Tiffany), Taiwan, and South Korea. India's bridal segment is growing but requires GIA certification education — clients in Tier 1–2 increasingly want GIA alongside hallmarking. In China, lab-grown diamond awareness requires careful selling — Aurelle does not offer lab-grown diamonds; the natural stone heritage narrative must be proactively led.

FINE JEWELLERY COLLECTIONS (SEASONAL)
Seasonal collections (typically 2 per year) featuring fashion-forward designs, colored stones, and experimental forms. Examples: Coloratura (vibrant sapphire, emerald, rubies in geometric settings), Étourdissant de Aurelle (celestial motifs). These serve as conversation-starters for existing clients and trend-driven acquisition for fashion-oriented prospects. Average price range: USD 3,000–USD 25,000.

title: Boutique Operations Standards & Client Experience Protocol
content:
Aurelle Boutique Operations Standards — APAC Client Experience Protocol

THE AURELLE CLIENT EXPERIENCE PHILOSOPHY
Every client who enters an Aurelle boutique enters what we call the 'Maison Reality' — a world that is deliberately removed from the commercial street outside. The experience must communicate one thing above all others: 'You are expected here, you are welcome here, and you will be understood here.' Operations standards exist not for compliance but to protect this promise consistently across 67 doors and 1,100 client-facing staff.

BOUTIQUE ENVIRONMENT STANDARDS

Ambiance & Sensory:
- Fragrance: Signature Aurelle ambient scent (La Panthère accord) diffused at consistent intensity — 8% concentration by air volume. Never masked by cleaning products (all cleaning done minimum 45 minutes before opening).
- Sound: Aurelle curated playlist (updated quarterly by Paris brand team) at maximum 45 decibels in retail floor. Private salons: no ambient sound unless client preference stated.
- Temperature: 20–22°C year-round. Any deviation must be corrected within 2 hours; if HVAC failure exceeds 4 hours, boutique director has authority to suspend VIC appointments with client notification.
- Lighting: LED lighting maintained at 85% of design specification. Jewellery display spotlights at 100%. Faulty spotlights must be replaced within 24 hours — dimly lit displays are a critical brand failure.
- Display: All display pieces presented on Aurelle-standard velvet trays, no fingerprints or marks on glass cases (wiped every 30 minutes during peak hours). Missing display pieces must be replaced with 'available upon request' card — never an empty slot.

Staff Presentation:
- Dress code: Aurelle-supplied professional attire (market-adapted — business formal in JP, KR; business contemporary in AU, SG). Hair: groomed, away from face. Fragrance: stylists wear La Panthère or Déclaration (complimentary) — never a competing luxury house fragrance.
- Name cards: Presented at first meeting with both hands (Japan, Korea, China) or single hand with eye contact (other markets) — culturally calibrated.
- Mobile phones: Never visible on the retail floor during working hours. Client communication devices (CRM tablets) only.

CLIENT GREETING & ENGAGEMENT PROTOCOL

First 30 Seconds (Critical Window):
- ALL clients greeted within 30 seconds of entry — regardless of apparent purchase intent or dressed-down appearance. The 'billionaire in jeans' principle: appearance is never a filter.
- Greeting standard: Eye contact, genuine smile, verbal welcome. Avoid: 'Can I help you?' (transactional) or 'Are you looking for something specific?' (presumptuous). Preferred: 'Welcome to Aurelle. Please take your time — I'm here whenever you'd like to explore together.'
- For returning clients: Recognition within 15 seconds if client is in system. Stylist retrieves client CRM profile within 30 seconds of greeting. Refer to name from first interaction point: 'Ms. Chen, it's wonderful to see you again. How are you?'

Discovery Conversation (Before Showing Product):
- Minimum 3 questions before moving to product: What is the occasion? (If applicable.) What category are they drawn to? Have they experienced Aurelle before?
- Listen for life events: birthdays, anniversaries, promotions, new baby, graduation, travel — these are relationship anchors to note in CRM.
- Never rush to the showcase. The conversation is the sale.

Product Presentation Standards:
- Pieces must be presented on a tray — never handed directly from showcase to client hand
- White gloves for pieces valued above USD 20,000 (jewellery). Watch presentations may be without gloves if it creates a more natural interaction.
- Describe the piece in this order: heritage story → design language → craftsmanship detail → material → price (price always last, never first)
- For watches: invite the client to wear the piece and look in the mirror. 'How does it feel on your wrist?' — physical experience seals emotional connection.
- For jewellery: always invite to try on. Have a mirror at the correct height for the piece (wrist mirror for bracelets, full-length for necklaces).

CLOSING & PURCHASE PROTOCOL
- Never pressure-close. The statement 'This piece is part of a very limited allocation and several clients have expressed interest' is only to be used when factually true and must be verified before stating.
- When a client is hesitant: 'Would you like me to take a note of this piece for you? I can reach out if any availability or collection changes occur.' (CRM wish-list function.)
- When a client purchases: Packaging is part of the experience — Aurelle box, tissue, ribbon applied in front of the client with care and deliberateness. Never rushed, never in the back room out of sight.
- Invite client to personalize if eligible (engraving, strap selection, etc.)
- Post-purchase: Confirm follow-up commitment: 'I'll reach out in a week to make sure you're happy with your piece. And if you ever have questions about care or servicing, please contact me directly.' Exchange personal stylist contact.

NPS & QUALITY MEASUREMENT
- Client NPS surveys sent 48 hours post-purchase (digital, maximum 3 questions)
- Mystery shopping: Minimum 2 mystery visits per boutique per quarter (unannounced, conducted by regional operations team or approved third-party firm)
- Mystery shopping scoring dimensions: Greeting (20%), product knowledge (25%), experience quality (30%), closing and follow-up (25%)
- Minimum acceptable mystery shop score: 82/100. Any boutique scoring below 75 triggers a Performance Improvement Plan with 60-day timeline.
- NPS target: 72 APAC average. Individual boutique NPS below 60 requires Market Director review.

COMPLAINT & ESCALATION PROTOCOL
- Level 1 (Stylist can resolve): Product question, minor service request, scheduling issue — resolve within same interaction
- Level 2 (Boutique Director resolves): Product quality concern, service dissatisfaction, pricing question — resolve within 24 hours, documented in CRM
- Level 3 (Market Director involved): Defective High Jewellery or watch, VIC relationship complaint, media risk — resolve within 4 hours response, full resolution within 5 business days
- Level 4 (APAC VP escalation): Potential legal issue, significant brand reputation risk, Tier 1 Icon client dispute — immediate APAC VP notification, legal team briefed within 12 hours

title: Client Segmentation & CRM Guidelines
content:
Aurelle APAC Client Segmentation Framework — Full Guidelines

SEGMENTATION PHILOSOPHY
At Aurelle, every client relationship is treated as a long-term investment, not a transactional event. The segmentation framework exists not to limit service but to guide the allocation of finite relationship resources (stylist time, event invitations, gifting budgets, travel) toward the greatest long-term mutual value. Tier assignment is dynamic and reviewed quarterly — clients move up and down based on trailing 24-month CLV recalculation.

TIER DEFINITIONS & ENTITLEMENTS

TIER 1 — MAISON VIP (CLV > USD 50,000 | APAC count: ~4,200 clients)
Profile: Ultra-high-net-worth individuals, second-generation wealth inheritors, C-suite executives, entertainment and cultural figures. Often own multiple Aurelle pieces across categories. Purchase frequency: minimum 2x per year. Strong emotional connection to the Maison.
Entitlements:
- Dedicated Personal Stylist assigned (named, consistent, no rotation without client consent)
- Priority access to all limited editions, pre-launches, and waitlisted references (minimum 72-hour window before any other tier)
- Annual bespoke gifting budget: USD 2,500 per client (approved via Market Director, recorded in CRM)
- Invitations to minimum 4 global events per year: Watches & Wonders Geneva, Paris High Jewellery Gala, SIHH equivalent, APAC regional Maison event
- Complimentary service: all watch services (battery, strap, bracelet adjustment), all jewellery cleaning and minor repairs
- Birthday recognition: physical gift delivered to preferred address, minimum value USD 300, must arrive within 3 days of birthday
- Target touchpoint frequency: minimum twice monthly (mix of in-person, curated message, event invitation, content sharing)
- Home or office viewings available for HJ and High Complications watches upon request
- Travel accompaniment service: when client travels to another APAC city, their home stylist notifies destination boutique, which prepares a personalized welcome

TIER 1A — MAISON ICON (CLV > USD 250,000 | subset of Tier 1 | APAC count: ~620 clients)
All Tier 1 entitlements PLUS:
- Personal relationship managed jointly by Stylist and Market Director
- Direct access to APAC VP Client Relations for any escalation
- Annual home visit by Gemologist or Watch Specialist for collection review and servicing
- Bespoke commission facilitation: direct introduction to Paris HQ atelier
- Gifting budget elevated to USD 5,000 annually
- Invitation to Aurelle Private Foundation events and brand heritage experiences (e.g., workshop visits)
- Unlimited complimentary alterations and servicing

TIER 2 — PREMIUM (CLV USD 10,000–50,000 | APAC count: ~18,500 clients)
Profile: Established professionals, entrepreneurs, dual-income households. Loyal purchasers with category depth in 1–2 families. Typically enter through watches or classic jewellery (LOVE, Trinity).
Entitlements:
- Shared stylist model: 1 stylist per 20 clients maximum (market directors may adjust ratio based on boutique capacity)
- In-boutique preview invitations for seasonal collections (not private Maison viewings — separate boutique evening events)
- Seasonal catalog mailed (physical, premium print)
- Birthday recognition: digital or boutique message with small in-boutique gift (scented candle, branded item, value USD 50–100)
- Target touchpoint frequency: monthly (mix of digital and in-person)
- Priority scheduling for boutique appointments (48-hour advance booking preferred)
- Upgrade pathway: explicitly communicated when client approaches Tier 1 threshold — stylist briefs Market Director for hand-off preparation

TIER 3 — ASPIRATIONAL (CLV USD 1,000–10,000 | APAC count: ~95,000 clients)
Profile: Young professionals, aspirational luxury entrants, gifting purchasers. Entry purchases typically: Trinity rings, LOVE bracelets (base models), fragrances, small leather goods, entry Santos or Tank watches.
Entitlements:
- Digital-led CRM: email newsletters, WeChat/LINE/KakaoTalk automated journeys with personalization tokens
- Curated product recommendations based on entry purchase category and browsing behavior (AI-powered recommendation engine active in CN, JP, KR, SG)
- Event invitations for boutique-wide seasonal events (open format, not private)
- Loyalty milestone recognition: acknowledgment at 1st, 3rd, and 5th purchase milestones
- Educational content: Aurelle heritage stories, craft videos, gemstone education — builds emotional connection and justifies price-value
- Upgrade pathway: CRM system flags clients within 20% of Tier 2 threshold for manual stylist outreach

TIER 0 — PROSPECT (No purchase | APAC count: active tracking ~220,000)
- Captured via boutique visit registration, digital sign-up, event attendance, referral program
- Automated welcome journey (3-email/message sequence over 30 days)
- Invitation to boutique open-house events and new collection launches
- First purchase incentive: complimentary engraving or personalization on eligible entry references

CHURN PREVENTION PROTOCOL
Risk Definition:
- LOW RISK: Last purchase <90 days ago — standard touchpoint cycle
- MEDIUM RISK: Last purchase 90–180 days ago — stylist prompted to initiate personal contact, no automated messages
- HIGH RISK: Last purchase 180–270 days ago — Market Director notified, personalized outreach required within 7 days, offer of exclusive preview or complimentary service
- CRITICAL RISK: Last purchase >270 days ago — Escalated to CRM Director and Market Director jointly. Win-back campaign initiated: personalized invitation to private event, bespoke offer (new collection first look), potentially Boutique Director personal call
- Lost: >540 days, no response to 3+ outreaches — moved to reactivation pool; approached maximum twice per year with high-value content only

Churn Context by Market (FY2024 actuals):
- Japan: lowest churn rate (8% annual) — relationship culture, long stylist tenure
- Australia: highest churn rate (24%) — more transactional market, lower emotional brand attachment, price sensitivity
- China: 14% — high competition from local and other luxury brands, digital engagement partially mitigates
- South Korea: 11% — strong brand loyalty once established, influenced by cultural brand prestige

CRM SYSTEM NOTES
Platform: Salesforce Luxury CRM (custom Aurelle instance) integrated with local platforms (WeChat Work for China, Line Business for JP/TH, KakaoTalk Business for KR). All client interactions must be logged within 24 hours of occurrence. Stylist notes field: minimum 3 lines of qualitative context per interaction (not just outcome — include client mood, mentioned life events, expressed preferences). These notes form the foundation of relationship continuity when stylist changes occur.

title: Digital & CRM Technology Infrastructure
content:
Aurelle APAC Digital & CRM Technology Infrastructure

TECHNOLOGY STACK OVERVIEW
Aurelle APAC operates a unified CRM and digital engagement stack that must balance: (1) global brand consistency managed from Paris HQ, (2) regional digital infrastructure requirements (especially China's separate internet ecosystem), and (3) local market cultural communication preferences.

CORE CRM PLATFORM
Platform: Salesforce CRM (Luxury Edition — custom Aurelle instance 'Maison Connect')
Key modules:
- Client 360 Profile: Full transactional history, category preferences, life events, communication preferences, stylist notes, wish-list, service history, event attendance record
- Touchpoint Scheduler: Automated prompts to stylists for upcoming birthdays, anniversaries, churn risk triggers, and product arrival aligned to client preferences
- Allocation Link: Direct connection to supply chain allocation engine — stylists can see real-time stock status and waitlist position for client wish-listed items
- Event Manager: Invitation management, RSVP tracking, event attendance history
- NPS Integration: Survey dispatch and response capture linked to transaction records
Data governance: Client data is stored in-market in compliance with local data protection regulations (China PIPL, Australia Privacy Act, South Korea PIPA, Japan APPI, Singapore PDPA, India PDPB). Cross-market client profile sharing requires documented client consent (collected at CRM registration).

CHINA DIGITAL ECOSYSTEM (SEPARATE STACK)
Due to the Great Firewall, China operates on a fully separate technology stack:
- WeChat Work (企业微信): Primary stylist-client communication tool replacing email and personal WeChat. All client conversations in China must be conducted via WeChat Work — ensures data compliance, CRM logging, and brand communication standards.
- WeChat Mini-Program (微信小程序): Client-facing discovery, appointment booking, product catalog browsing, wish-list creation, and loyalty program interaction. 8M+ followers. Mini-Program purchases (click-to-boutique) feed directly into Salesforce CN instance.
- Salesforce CN Instance: Separate data instance hosted on Alibaba Cloud (compliant with PIPL). Syncs anonymized analytics with global instance but does not share personally identifiable information without explicit consent.
- RED (Xiaohongshu): Brand content publishing — managed by CN marketing team. Stylists may not post from personal accounts without brand approval (brand protection policy).
- Local Analytics: Tencent Analytics for WeChat behavior, Alibaba Analytics for e-commerce adjacent signals.

JAPAN DIGITAL
- LINE Official Account: Primary CRM communication channel. Aurelle Japan LINE account has 1.2M followers (large but broad — conversion from follower to purchaser is the key metric, targeted at 2.1% in FY2025).
- LINE CRM Automation: Segmented messaging flows by tier — Tier 1 receives stylist-personalized messages via LINE, Tiers 2 and 3 receive automated but personalized content via LINE broadcast.
- Email: Still used for formal communications (event invitations, service receipts) alongside LINE.

SOUTH KOREA DIGITAL
- KakaoTalk Business Channel: Primary communication. Aurelle KR KakaoTalk channel has 890K subscribers. Purchase inquiry via KakaoTalk is common — response time SLA: 30 minutes during business hours for Tier 1 and 2 clients.
- Instagram: Strong brand discovery platform for younger segments (25–35). Content managed by KR marketing team. Stylist resharing of approved content is encouraged.

SOUTHEAST ASIA & INDIA
- Email remains dominant for formal CRM (SG, AU, IN, PH)
- WhatsApp Business: Used in India, Philippines, and Thailand for client communication. Aurelle has a WhatsApp Business API integration for message templates and CRM logging. Personal stylist WhatsApp number sharing with clients is permitted but all transactions and commitments must be logged in Salesforce within 4 hours.
- LINE: Thailand and Singapore have LINE user bases (particularly Thai market).

AURELLE CLIENT APP (NEW FY2025)
Launching Q1 2025 for markets outside China: iOS and Android. Features: Personal style profile, wish-list management, piece authentication (NFC tag on each piece connects to digital certificate of authenticity), service appointment booking, direct stylist messaging (in-app, CRM-connected), event invitations and RSVP. Initial launch markets: Singapore, Hong Kong, Australia, South Korea. Rollout to remaining ex-China markets Q3 2025.
Privacy-first architecture: No behavioral advertising, no data selling. Client data used exclusively for relationship management. This must be communicated clearly — luxury clients are privacy-sensitive.

DIGITAL COMMUNICATION STANDARDS
- Response time SLA (all channels):
  Tier 1 Icon: 30 minutes during business hours, 2 hours outside
  Tier 1 VIP: 1 hour business hours, 4 hours outside
  Tier 2: 4 hours business hours
  Tier 3: 24 hours
- Tone: All digital communications must maintain Aurelle voice — warm, elegant, knowledgeable, personalized. No emojis except in markets where cultural context makes them appropriate (informal closing in JP/KR acceptable). No abbreviations. No copy-paste templates visible as templates.
- Message frequency: Maximum 2 proactive outreaches per month for Tier 2 and 3 (excluding event invitations and service notifications). Tier 1: as relationship warrants — no maximum, but quality over frequency.
- Language: Communicate in client's preferred language. Mandarin Simplified (CN clients), Mandarin Traditional (TW, HK), Japanese, Korean, English, Thai. Stylists must not communicate in a language they are not fluent in — use regional CRM team support for language gaps.

AI & PERSONALIZATION CAPABILITIES (FY2025)
The APAC CRM team is piloting AI-powered personalization tools:
- Recommendation Engine: Analyzes purchase history, browsing behavior (with consent), and peer-cluster behavior to suggest relevant pieces for stylist to present during outreach. Currently live in CN, JP, KR, SG.
- Churn Prediction Model: Machine learning model trained on APAC transactional data to predict churn probability 60 days before threshold — allows proactive intervention. F1 score: 0.78 (FY2024 validation). Being refined with FY2024 full-year data.
- Sentiment Analysis: Applied to CRM stylist notes (with appropriate privacy controls) to identify patterns in client satisfaction and flag potential issues before formal complaint.
- Gen AI Drafting Tool: Stylists in pilot markets (SG, AU, HK) can use an AI drafting assistant (built on Aurelle's internal LLM, not connected to public internet) to draft personalized outreach messages — stylist reviews and edits before sending. Reduces drafting time by 65% while maintaining personalization.

title: Event Strategy & Client Experience Programming APAC
content:
Aurelle APAC Event Strategy & Client Experience Programming FY2025

STRATEGIC ROLE OF EVENTS
Events are not marketing expenses — they are the highest-ROI investment in client relationship capital available to Aurelle. Analysis of FY2024 data demonstrates:
- Clients who attended a Maison private event in the previous 12 months have a 78% repeat purchase rate vs. 54% baseline
- Average transaction value in the 90 days post-event is 2.3x higher than non-event periods
- Event attendees have a 67% lower churn rate than non-attendees in the same CLV tier
- RSVP acceptance to purchase conversion: 41% at High Jewellery private viewings, 28% at watch events, 19% at brand experience events (lower conversion but highest relationship depth)

The FY2025 APAC event budget: USD 8.4M (1.35% of revenue target). Allocated: 45% HJ events, 25% watch events, 20% brand experience events, 10% digital/hybrid events.

EVENT TYPOLOGY & STANDARDS

TYPE 1 — HIGH JEWELLERY PRIVATE VIEWINGS
(As governed by DOC-002 — see for full protocol)
Frequency: 4 per year per flagship market (aligned with collection calendar)
